symptom: a belt that kept feeling tighter that got blamed on a creeping beer gut
hook: the spleen had quietly grown into a solid organ the size of a football crushing everything around it
whatYouSee: One smooth white blob swallows almost the whole left side of the belly and pushes every gas-filled gut loop into a thin crescent in the right corner.
whyItMatters: A normal spleen is fist-sized and tucked under the ribs where a flat film never shows it. Blood and marrow diseases can drive it to swell ten to twenty times normal and one knock can rupture it into a fatal internal bleed.
treatment: Doctors hunt the cause with blood counts and a marrow biopsy then treat the underlying disease. In severe cases the spleen is removed surgically followed by lifelong vaccines to cover the lost organ.
takeaway: An organ the size of your fist can silently balloon into a football and the only clue is a belt that suddenly will not close.
igOptions: B. A giant spleen grown 15 times normal
C. A stomach full of trapped gas

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a belt that kept feeling tighter that got blamed on a creeping beer gut.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And the spleen had quietly grown into a solid organ the size of a football crushing everything around it.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Massive splenomegaly

👀 What you see:
One smooth white blob swallows almost the whole left side of the belly and pushes every gas-filled gut loop into a thin crescent in the right corner.

🦴 Why it matters:
A normal spleen is fist-sized and tucked under the ribs where a flat film never shows it. Blood and marrow diseases can drive it to swell ten to twenty times normal and one knock can rupture it into a fatal internal bleed.
